items.title: Excessive Any typing on core interfaces
items.description: 30+ uses of Any across core modules. RegisteredTool.execute typed Any instead of Callable. vault_resolver is Any in 3 locations. ModuleBus and ToolRegistry accept telemetry: Any. ArcAgent.run() returns Any.

items.title: Missing Protocol/ABC for core component interfaces
items.description: Only Module uses Protocol. Missing: TelemetryProtocol, VaultResolverProtocol, ToolExecutorProtocol, ContextTransformerProtocol. Violates build standard: Interfaces over implementations.

items.title: Broad except Exception catches without specificity
items.description: 6 instances of bare except Exception. config.py should catch ValidationError. module_bus.py should not swallow CancelledError. identity.py vault catch is too broad.

items.title: Empty security and performance test directories
items.description: tests/security/ and tests/performance/ exist but are empty. No tests for key permission validation, DID format injection, tool argument sanitization, audit log integrity, or cold-start benchmarks.

items.title: Telemetry recreated instead of updated during startup
items.description: AgentTelemetry constructed twice during ArcAgent.startup() -- first with pending DID, then reconstructed with real DID. Orphans first tracer instance.
items.notes: S003 review: startup now uses set_agent_did() instead of reconstruction. TD partially addressed but set_agent_did was not verified as updating trace provider.

items.title: Native tool registration lacks input schema introspection
items.description: register_native_tools() creates tools with empty schema regardless of function parameters. LLM gets no parameter information, degrading tool-use accuracy.

items.title: Context manager compact threshold has no implementation
items.description: compact_threshold (85%) defined but LLM summarization strategy not implemented. Gap between prune (70%) and emergency (95%) has no graduated response. Documented as Phase 3.

items.title: type: ignore[arg-type] in module lifecycle tests
items.description: 6 instances of type: ignore due to Module Protocol requiring @property for name. Test classes use class attributes which don't satisfy the Protocol.

items.title: No audit event on error paths in tool execution
items.description: wrapped_execute emits audit events only on success. Timeout errors and veto rejections produce no audit trail. Violates NIST 800-53 AU-2, AU-3. Compounded by TD-016 where built-in tools return error strings that bypass audit entirely.

items.title: No timestamp or sequence number in audit events
items.description: audit_event() emits event_type, agent_did, and details but no timestamp, sequence number, or session ID. Violates NIST 800-53 AU-8 (Time Stamps) and AU-10 (Non-Repudiation).

items.title: MCP/HTTP/Process tool transports not implemented
items.description: Config models exist for all 4 transports but only native registration is implemented. MCP, HTTP, process have no registration or execution logic.

items.title: Bridge pattern fire-and-forget creates unobserved failures
items.description: create_arcrun_bridge() schedules bus.emit() as fire-and-forget tasks. Failures silently swallowed. Tests only verify bridge is callable, not event delivery.

items.title: Integration tests access private members extensively
items.description: Tests directly access agent._started, agent._telemetry, agent._identity, agent._bus, agent._tool_registry, agent._context, agent._telemetry._agent_did. Couples tests to implementation.

items.title: DID identifier uses only 8 hex characters (32 bits of entropy)
items.description: SHA-256 truncated to 8 hex chars. At 10,000 agents, birthday paradox gives ~1% collision probability. Should use 16 hex chars for practical collision immunity.

items.title: Synchronous file I/O in async tool functions
items.description: Path.read_text(), Path.write_text(), Path.exists(), Path.is_file(), and Path.mkdir() are synchronous blocking calls inside async def execute() functions. Blocks the entire event loop. With 1000 concurrent agents, a 50ms disk read blocks all other agents. Violates build standard: Async-first.
items.notes: S003 review: Extended by TD-036 (grep/find/ls have same issue). Remediate together.

items.title: Dual error signaling pattern (ToolError exceptions vs error strings)
items.description: _validation.py raises ToolError (structured, auditable). read.py, write.py, edit.py return plain 'Error: ...' strings that pass through as successful tool results. No audit event emitted. Callers cannot distinguish errors from content. Compounds TD-009. Violates NIST 800-53 AU-2.
items.notes: S003 review: grep/find/ls follow same pattern (returning error strings). Scope expanded.

items.title: No configuration to disable or select built-in tools
items.description: All four built-in tools (read, write, edit, bash) are unconditionally registered in agent.py startup. No config to disable bash for read-only agents. ToolConfig.deny crashes startup instead of gracefully skipping. Violates least privilege (NIST 800-53 AC-6, OWASP ASI03).
items.notes: S003 review: Now 7 built-in tools (read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls). Scope increased.

items.title: Input schemas defined as raw dicts instead of Pydantic models
items.description: Each tool defines INPUT_SCHEMA as hand-written dict[str, Any]. No validation that schema matches function signature. file_path field duplicated across 3 tools identically. Violates build standard: Pydantic models for all data boundaries, DRY.
items.location: arcagent/tools/read.py:15-38, arcagent/tools/write.py:14-30, arcagent/tools/edit.py:15-42, arcagent/tools/bash.py:18-31, arcagent/tools/grep.py:20-50, arcagent/tools/find.py:17-40, arcagent/tools/ls.py:15-26
items.notes: S003 review: 3 more tools with raw dict schemas (grep, find, ls). Scope expanded to 7 tools.

items.title: No BaseTool protocol or abstract interface for tool modules
items.description: Each tool module exports create_tool(workspace) -> RegisteredTool by convention only. No Protocol or ABC defines the contract. Cannot type-check tool modules at import. Cannot discover tools automatically. Compounds TD-002.

items.title: Hardcoded tool list in __init__.py won't scale
items.description: create_builtin_tools() hardcodes exactly 4 imports. Adding a 5th tool requires modifying __init__.py. Closed extension point violates open/closed principle. Creates merge conflicts when multiple tools added in parallel.
items.notes: S003 review: Now 7 hardcoded imports. Problem worsened as predicted.

items.title: Bash tool has no command allowlist/denylist or sandboxing
items.description: Command string passed directly to asyncio.create_subprocess_shell() with no filtering. LLM can execute arbitrary commands outside workspace (rm -rf /, curl exfiltration, read /etc/passwd). Workspace cwd is not a security boundary. Largest attack surface in built-in tools. OWASP LLM05, LLM06, ASI02, ASI05.

items.title: Zero unit tests for built-in tools
items.description: No tests/unit/tools/ directory. Zero coverage on path traversal prevention, read offset/limit edge cases, write directory creation, edit uniqueness, bash timeout/truncation, all error paths. Compounds TD-004. Violates quality gate: >=90% for core components.
items.resolution: Added 66 unit tests across 6 test files in tests/unit/tools/. Coverage at 98.88%. Covers core functionality, security hardening (symlinks, binary files, null bytes, file size limits, empty old_string), edge cases, and factory metadata.

items.title: **_kwargs: Any swallows unexpected arguments silently
items.description: Every tool execute function accepts **_kwargs: Any, silently discarding hallucinated LLM parameters. Masks bugs and prevents mypy from catching argument errors at boundary. Should log or strip via schema.
items.notes: S003 review: Extended by TD-038 (grep/find/ls have same pattern). Remediate together.

items.title: Write tool reports bytes but measures characters
items.description: len(content) returns character count, not byte count. Message says 'bytes'. For multi-byte UTF-8 characters the reported size is wrong. Minor but technically incorrect for audit logs.
items.resolution: Changed len(content) to len(content.encode('utf-8')) so reported size matches actual bytes written.

items.title: ADR needed: Bash security model (sandboxing strategy)
items.description: Bash tool executes arbitrary shell commands with no filtering, allowlist, or sandbox. Need an ADR documenting the security model: whether to use namespace isolation, command allowlists, chroot jails, or policy-based filtering. References TD-021, OWASP ASI02/ASI05/LLM05/LLM06.

items.title: ADR needed: Tool override policy (user tools vs built-in)
items.description: Built-in tools are registered before user-configured native tools. If a user registers a tool with the same name as a built-in, the behavior is undefined. Need an ADR documenting: can users override built-ins? What happens on name collision? Should built-ins be final?
items.notes: S003 review: Extensions can also register tools with same name as built-ins. Three collision points now: built-in, config native, extensions.

items.title: ADR needed: Error handling convention (ToolError vs strings)
items.description: Validation raises ToolError (structured, auditable). Tool execute functions return 'Error: ...' strings (unstructured, bypass audit). Need an ADR deciding: should all tool errors raise ToolError? Should strings be reserved for user-facing messages? References TD-016, TD-009.

items.title: ADR needed: Parameter runtime validation strategy
items.description: Tool input schemas are raw dicts, not Pydantic models. LLM-provided parameters are not validated against the schema before execute() is called. Need an ADR deciding: should the ToolRegistry validate inputs against schema before calling execute? Should tools use Pydantic for input parsing? References TD-018.

items.title: _remove_extension_handlers accesses bus._handlers directly
items.description: _remove_extension_handlers() iterates and mutates bus._handlers (a private defaultdict) directly. Bypasses future handler lifecycle hooks, breaks if ModuleBus changes internal structure, and violates build standard 'Interfaces over implementations'. Test fixture also manually constructs bus._handlers confirming the coupling.

items.title: 'paths' sandbox mode defined in config/SDD but not implemented
items.description: ExtensionEntry.sandbox_mode documents 'workspace | paths | strict' but _load_extension only branches on 'strict' vs everything else. Extension configured with sandbox_mode='paths' silently runs as 'workspace' (unrestricted). Silent security degradation -- operator thinks they have path restrictions but nothing is enforced.

items.title: Code duplication between _load_extension and _load_entry_points
items.description: _load_extension and _load_entry_points share ~80 lines of identical logic: ExtensionAPI creation, factory invocation, ExtensionManifest building, audit event emission, logging. Only differences are module loading mechanism, sandbox mode, and source label. DRY violation -- bug fix in one path could be missed in the other.

items.title: Strict sandbox monkey-patches builtins.open globally -- not thread-safe
items.description: _strict_sandbox patches builtins.open and subprocess.run/Popen globally as context manager. If two extensions load concurrently or factory yields to event loop, one factory's sandbox bleeds into another. Extension A in 'workspace' mode could be silently sandboxed during Extension B's 'strict' load. type: ignore comments on lines 432, 437, 450 are justified.

items.title: settings_manager._persist_to_toml uses regex for TOML serialization
items.description: Since tomllib is read-only, settings manager uses re.sub with pattern to replace [settings] section. Breaks if setting value contains '[', does not handle inline comments, does not escape TOML special characters, does not handle multiline values. Python str() used for int/float differs from TOML spec for edge cases.

items.title: Core LOC at 3,269 -- 9% over 3,000 budget
items.description: Build standard mandates core under 3,000 LOC. Current breakdown: agent.py (566), extensions.py (450), config.py (304), tool_registry.py (293), session_manager.py (257), context_manager.py (232), errors.py (215), identity.py (214), module_bus.py (194), settings_manager.py (186), skill_registry.py (160), telemetry.py (145), protocols.py (52). PLAN.md identified session_manager.py as extraction candidate.

items.title: extensions.py at 450 LOC -- 50% over 300 target
items.description: Planning target was 300 LOC for extensions. At 450 LOC with sandbox logic (54 LOC) and entry point discovery (12 LOC) as extraction candidates. Combined with TD-031 deduplication, could reach 300-350 LOC.

items.title: Synchronous file I/O in grep, find, and ls tool execute functions
items.description: Same issue as TD-015 extended to new tools. grep reads potentially thousands of files synchronously inside async function. find calls glob() and stat() synchronously. ls calls iterdir() and stat(). With 1000 concurrent agents, blocks event loop for hundreds of milliseconds.

items.title: Grep tool compiles user-supplied regex without complexity limits
items.description: Grep compiles LLM-provided regex and applies to potentially millions of lines. Catastrophic backtracking patterns like (a+)+$ hang event loop. _MAX_FILE_SIZE (5MB) and _DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS (100) mitigate but single 5MB file with malicious pattern can still block for minutes. OWASP ASI02 (Tool Misuse).

items.title: **_kwargs: Any in grep, find, ls tool functions
items.description: Same issue as TD-023 extended to 3 new tools. Hallucinated LLM parameters silently discarded. Should be resolved together with TD-023.

items.title: Unit tests access private agent._* members extensively
items.description: test_agent.py directly accesses agent._started, agent._telemetry, agent._identity, agent._bus, agent._tool_registry, agent._context, agent._session, agent._model. test_settings_manager.py sets sm._overlay. Couples tests to implementation. Extends TD-013.

items.title: agent.py duplicates workspace resolution 3 times
items.description: Path(self._config.agent.workspace).resolve() appears at lines 202 and 221 in startup(). Path(self._config.agent.workspace) without .resolve() at lines 302 and 387 in run() and chat(). Inconsistent use of .resolve() is a bug waiting to happen with symlinks.

items.title: SkillRegistry._extract_frontmatter does not sanitize for prompt injection
items.description: Skills in _agent-created/ are LLM-authored. Parsed YAML data flows into SkillMeta with minimal validation. Crafted skill name containing newlines or XML characters could inject content into system prompt via format_for_prompt() which builds XML tags with f-strings. OWASP LLM01 (Prompt Injection), ASI06 (Memory & Context Poisoning).

items.title: find tool uses glob() vs grep uses rglob() -- inconsistent recursion
items.description: grep uses search_root.rglob(glob_filter) for recursive search. find uses search_root.glob(pattern) which only recurses if pattern starts with **. Passing *.py to find gets top-level only; passing *.py to grep's glob_filter gets all files recursively. Inconsistency will confuse LLM.

history.title: Zero unit tests for built-in tools
Write tool reports bytes but measures characters

S003_Phase_1b.typeIgnoreAudit: 3 type: ignore in extensions.py -- all justified (security sandbox builtins patching + Python 3.9-3.11 compat)
S003_Phase_1b.topPriority: TD-029 (bus._handlers encapsulation) and TD-036 (sync I/O in new tools) should be addressed immediately
S003_Phase_1b.coreLocTrend: 1816 -> 3269 (+80%). Budget exceeded. session_manager.py (257 LOC) + skill_registry.py (160 LOC) are extraction candidates.
batchOpportunities: TD-015 + TD-036: Unified async I/O remediation
